typed strict.description: # typed: strict — all methods must have sigs, no untyped

typed true.description: # typed: true — type-checks the file, untyped values allowed

typed false.description: # typed: false — opts file out of type checking

sig returns.description: sig { returns(T) } — method with no params and a return type

sig params returns.description: sig { params(...).returns(T) } — method with params and return type

sig params void.description: sig { params(...).void } — method with params that returns nothing

T.nilable.description: T.nilable(T) — type that is T or nil

T.let.description: T.let(value, T) — assert and bind a local variable to a type

T.cast.description: T.cast(value, T) — forcefully cast a value to a type (unchecked)

T.must.description: T.must(value) — assert value is not nil, raises at runtime if it is

T.unsafe.description: T.unsafe(value) — escape hatch, treats value as T.untyped

T.any.description: T.any(T1, T2) — union type, value can be any of the listed types

T.all.description: T.all(T1, T2) — intersection type, value must satisfy all types

T::Enum.description: T::Enum subclass — runtime-checked enum with typed values

T::Struct.description: T::Struct subclass — immutable typed value object

abstract method.description: sig { abstract.returns(T) } — abstract method declaration (must be overridden)

override method.description: sig { override.returns(T) } — marks method as overriding a parent abstract/interface method

include T::Sig.description: include T::Sig — enables sig {} blocks in the class

include T::Helpers.description: include T::Helpers — enables abstract!, interface!, mixes_in_class_methods, etc.
